speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. Let's start by checking your vital signs — your temperature today is 37.1 °C, which is within normal range.

speaker: patient
text: Good morning, Doctor. Thanks for seeing me. I’ve been feeling a bit off lately.

speaker: doctor
text: I see you’re here for a routine follow-up. Any new symptoms or concerns since your last visit?

speaker: doctor
text: Your fasting blood glucose this morning is 6.2 mmol/L — slightly elevated but not alarming yet.

speaker: patient
text: Yeah, I’ve noticed some fatigue and occasional headaches in the afternoons.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure is 82 mmHg — well within target range for someone with hypertension.

speaker: doctor
text: You're still on Metformin 500mg twice daily — how’s that been tolerated? Any GI side effects?

speaker: doctor
text: Systolic pressure is 128 mmHg — nice and controlled. That’s reassuring.

speaker: patient
text: The Metformin’s been okay, though I did forget one dose last Tuesday. Otherwise, no stomach issues.

speaker: patient
text: Actually, I’ve had episodes of dizziness — especially when standing up quickly — over the past few days.

speaker: doctor
text: Dizziness on orthostasis — that’s important. Let’s check your BP again after you stand, and review your meds and hydration.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been drinking more water, and my urination hasn’t changed much. No chest pain or palpitations.

speaker: doctor
text: We’ll also want to rule out hypoglycemia or medication interactions — your glucose was stable this morning, but let’s monitor closely.

speaker: patient
text: I checked my sugar at home yesterday evening — it was 5.8, so not low. But I did feel lightheaded after dinner.

speaker: doctor
text: Given the dizziness and your BP readings, we may need to adjust your antihypertensive therapy — let’s take another set of vitals now.

speaker: patient
text: My temperature feels normal — same as when you first checked, that reading of 37.1 °C.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m going to switch you from your current regimen to Amlodipine 10mg once daily — it’s better tolerated and less likely to cause orthostatic dizziness than some alternatives.

speaker: doctor
text: Hmm — your temperature just came back at 37.7 °C. That’s a mild elevation compared to the 37.1 °C we saw earlier.

speaker: doctor
text: And your diastolic pressure is now 74 mmHg — lower than the 82 mmHg we recorded before. That could explain the dizziness.

speaker: patient
text: Wait — that 74 seems low. My other reading today was definitely 82 mmHg, the one you noted earlier.
